status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A task that needs to be done.
issuetype.name: Task

fields.description:
Currently, when we stop the parent queue, we would do the following:
1) check the number of active applications. If there is no active application, we would mark the queue state as STOPPED. Otherwise, we mark the queue state as DRAIN, and wait for all the active applications finish.
2) Do the same step for all its children.

So, in current process, we always change the state for parent queue first which will not work in some scenarios, such as the reservation system. In the reservation system, the plan queue will not have any active applications immediately at the beginning. So, when we stop the queue, it would change the queue state into STOPPED immediately which could cause problem.

fields.summary: make DELETE/STOP/CONVERT queues work in reservation system

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

comments.body:
To fix the issue, we need to change the process a little bit:
1) alway change the queue state to DRAIN for the parent queue.
2) for the leaf queue, check the number of active applications. If the number is 0, we would move to STOPPED state and inform its parent.
3) The parent queue got the update state from its children, it will move to STOPPED state if all its children are in STOPPED state.

Note: we will not handle plan queue here. The fix for the plan queue would be in : https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-6431
No new test cases included and make sure we could pass all previous test cases
